description_long: This is a red wooden traffic barricade with a weathered, painted finish, commonly used for access control in urban environments. The main horizontal plank is supported by two A-frame legs on either side, providing stability and visibility. The front face displays the bold white text 'NO PASAR' and 'DEPARTAMENTO MUNICIPAL DE BOMBEROS', indicating it is used by municipal fire departments to restrict passage. The structure is constructed from thick wooden planks, joined together with visible cross-bracing for added strength. The surface shows signs of wear, with some areas of faded paint and exposed wood grain. There are no visible reflective stripes, lights, or attachment fixtures, and the design is purely functional for temporary placement.
description: A red, weathered wooden traffic barricade with white warning text and sturdy A-frame supports, used to block passage in urban areas. The surface is rough and shows signs of use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the barricade, with clear white warning text and both A-frame supports visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the triangular A-frame structure and cross-bracing, with the barricade's thickness and construction details visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the back of the horizontal plank and A-frame supports, but without visible text.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, displaying the A-frame support and cross-bracing from the other side.

attribute_warning_signage.content: NO PASAR DEPARTAMENTO MUNICIPAL DE BOMBEROS
